questions.question: What happens to data inside a container when the container is removed?
options: The data is automatically backed up to Docker Hub
The data is moved to the host filesystem
The data is saved to the Docker daemon cache

questions.question: What term describes the temporary nature of containers?
Which two mechanisms does Docker provide for persisting data?
What command creates a named Docker volume called 'my-data'?
In the command 'docker run -v my-data:/app/data my-app', what does 'my-data' refer to?
Who manages the storage location of a named volume?
What is a bind mount?
options: A mapping of a specific host directory into the container
A read-only layer in a Docker image
questions.answer: A mapping of a specific host directory into the container

questions.question: In the command 'docker run -v $(pwd):/app my-app', what does '$(pwd)' represent?
Which type of mount is best suited for development with live code editing?
Which type of mount is best suited for database persistent data?
What happens to the contents of a container directory when a bind mount is attached to it?
options: The container directory contents are merged with the host directory
The host directory contents overwrite what was in the container directory
The container directory contents are copied to the host
Nothing changes; both directories remain independent
questions.answer: The host directory contents overwrite what was in the container directory

questions.question: What happens when a named volume is mounted to a container directory that already has data?
options: The volume overwrites the container data
The volume is pre-populated with the container directory's existing data
questions.answer: The volume is pre-populated with the container directory's existing data

questions.question: What command lists all Docker volumes on the system?
What command shows detailed information about a specific volume?
What command removes a specific named volume?
What command removes all unused Docker volumes?
When using bind mounts for development, what happens when you edit a file on the host?
options: You must rebuild the image for changes to take effect
You must restart the container for changes to take effect
The change is instantly visible inside the container
The change is queued until the container is stopped
questions.answer: The change is instantly visible inside the container

questions.question: In the command 'docker run -d -p 8080:80 -v $(pwd):/usr/share/nginx/html nginx', what is '/usr/share/nginx/html'?
options: The mount point inside the container where Nginx serves files
questions.answer: The mount point inside the container where Nginx serves files

questions.question: What is the default data directory for PostgreSQL inside its official Docker image?
If you run a PostgreSQL container without a volume, create a table, then remove and recreate the container, what happens to the table?
options: The table persists because PostgreSQL saves data to disk
The table is lost because the container's filesystem was destroyed
The table is automatically backed up by Docker
The table is moved to a temporary volume
questions.answer: The table is lost because the container's filesystem was destroyed

questions.question: If you run a PostgreSQL container with a named volume on /var/lib/postgresql/data, remove the container, and start a new one with the same volume, what happens to the data?
options: The data is lost because the container was removed
The data survives because it is stored in the volume, not the container
The data is corrupted because two containers used the same volume
The data must be manually restored from a backup
questions.answer: The data survives because it is stored in the volume, not the container

questions.question: What does the '--rm' flag do in 'docker run --rm -v app-data:/data alpine cat /data/message.txt'?
options: Removes the volume after the container exits
Removes the image after the container exits
Automatically removes the container when it exits
Removes all stopped containers before running
questions.answer: Automatically removes the container when it exits

questions.question: Can multiple containers mount the same named volume simultaneously?
options: No, only one container can use a volume at a time
Yes, multiple containers can share the same volume
Only if the volume is set to shared mode
Only if the containers are on the same network
questions.answer: Yes, multiple containers can share the same volume

questions.question: Which is more portable across different host machines: named volumes or bind mounts?
options: Bind mounts, because they use absolute paths
Named volumes, because they work everywhere without host-specific paths
Neither is portable; data must be manually transferred
questions.answer: Named volumes, because they work everywhere without host-specific paths

questions.question: Can you remove a volume that is currently in use by a running container?
options: Yes, but only with the --force flag
No, you must stop and remove the container first
No, but Docker will queue the removal for later
questions.answer: No, you must stop and remove the container first
